title: suaeda fruticosa var. longifolia
intro: Suaeda fruticosa var. longifolia, also known as the Long-leaved Glasswort, is a type of succulent plant that is native to coastal areas in Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Suaeda fruticosa var. longifolia include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, and can be made into a salve or ointment to apply topically to affected areas.
